reason: Rubric failures are test quality issues, not instruction issues. ALL fail-to-pass tests (test_monorepo_sets_packagemanager_for_bun through test_comment_updated) are pure string-matching that never execute code. They check for exact gold implementation strings: 'Monorepo templates use turbo which requires packageManager', 'function getPackageManagerVersion', 'if (isMonorepo)', 'execa(packageManager, ["--version"])', '`${packageManager}@', etc. A stub writing these as dead comments/functions would pass. Per the rubric: 'tests_verify_behavior_not_text' (all f2p tests are text inspection), 'test_not_tautological' (stub would pass), 'anti_cheating_measures' (tests only check string presence), and 'solution_uniqueness_guard' (tests assert gold-specific details) are all test problems. The instruction cannot be made more specific without violating 'no_solution_leakage' (adding literal strings the tests check for = solution leakage). The instruction also cannot be made less specific without violating 'behavior_in_task_description'. Oracle is intact (nop=0, gold=1).
